description_long: This hammer features a robust, cylindrical wooden head with a natural wood grain pattern and a slightly tapered shape. The head is solid and lacks any visible claws or peen, suggesting it is a mallet-style hammer, ideal for striking without marring surfaces. The handle is straight and wrapped with a textured grip, likely for improved handling and comfort during use. The wood displays a warm brown color with some reddish hues, and the grip area is a muted gray, possibly rubber or wrapped cord. There are no visible brand markings, logos, or decorative elements on the hammer. The overall appearance is clean and well-maintained, with no signs of wear or damage.
description: A wooden mallet-style hammer with a cylindrical head and a straight, textured grip handle. The wood grain and reddish-brown tones give it a sturdy, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: The hammer is seen from the front, showing the broad, rounded face of the wooden head and the textured grip on the handle.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the cylindrical shape of the head and the subtle wood grain patterns, with the grip wrapping visible on the lower handle.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the full length of the handle and the head, with more pronounced reddish wood grain streaks visible.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the smooth, continuous surface of the head and the grip, with no additional features or markings.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a textured, likely rubberized grip wrapping for enhanced hold.
attribute_head_type: Solid cylindrical wooden head, mallet-style, with no claws or peen; both ends are flat striking faces.
